contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Diagnose + fix Edgar Google OAuth (Sign-in with Google failing post-migration)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/sentiment_importer/pull/1054
  
  Details:
  Gary couldn't sign in to https://edgar.truesight.me/users/sign_in via Google. Google was returning 'Access blocked: Authorization Error / Error 400: invalid_request / doesn't comply with Google's OAuth 2.0 policy for keeping apps secure' — the generic policy banner that masks the actual reason.
  
  Diagnosis path:
  1. First read of the symptom suggested the OAuth consent screen's homepage / privacy URLs were dead (https://getdata.io returned HTTP 000 — connection refused). Steered Gary to console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials/consent for project 667737028020 (the legacy GetData.IO GCP project that owns the OAuth client krake_ror_prod, which Edgar reuses).
  2. Gary checked the OAuth Client page first (where redirect URIs and JS origins live) — those were correctly configured: https://edgar.truesight.me + https://edgar.truesight.me/users/auth/google_oauth2/callback both present. 'Last used date: May 6, 2026' confirmed the client was working as recently as 5 days ago — so the client config wasn't the problem.
  3. Asked Gary to click 'see error details' link on the Google error page. The actual error was: 'redirect_uri=/users/auth/google_oauth2/callback flowName=GeneralOAuthFlow'. Critical insight — the redirect_uri being SENT to Google was a RELATIVE PATH with no host. A relative path can never match the absolute https://edgar.truesight.me/... URLs registered on the OAuth client, so Google rejects with the policy banner.
  4. Verified the infrastructure side was clean:
     - DNS edgar.truesight.me -> 3.90.179.151 (autopilot seni_ror_new) ✓
     - Authoritative Route53 record matches ✓
     - nginx config on seni_ror_new sets proxy_set_header Host  + X-Forwarded-Proto https ✓
     - config/environments/production.rb has google_client_id + google_client_secret correctly set
     - Edgar service active, port 3002 listening ✓
     So nginx wasn't dropping headers — the issue had to be on the Rails side.
  5. Found the gap: OmniAuth was relying on Rack request inspection to construct the full URL, and that fell through to a path-only result behind the new nginx setup. Devise/omniauth-google-oauth2 doesn't auto-set OmniAuth.config.full_host; you must do it explicitly OR set config.action_controller.default_url_options. Edgar had only config.action_mailer.default_url_options set (for email URL helpers), which doesn't help OmniAuth.
  
  Sentiment_importer PR #1054 (merged + deployed):
  - New config/initializers/omniauth.rb sets OmniAuth.config.full_host = ENV['EDGAR_FULL_HOST'].presence || 'https://edgar.truesight.me' in production. Override-able via env var for a future staging environment.
  - Ran ./deploy.sh --skip-migrate against seni_ror_new + seni_sk_new; both came up cleanly (port 3002 healthy, Sidekiq active).
  - Bypasses Rack header inspection entirely so the OAuth flow no longer depends on subtle nginx forwarding behavior — robust against future infra changes.
  
  Separate concerns surfaced during diagnosis (not addressed in this PR — flagged for follow-up):
  1. Edgar runs as 'rails s -e production' (single-threaded WEBrick). nginx error log on seni_ror_new shows clusters of 'upstream prematurely closed connection' 502s — bot scan traffic on /companies/STWD and other heavy routes is choking the single-process server. Worth migrating to Puma + workers (the Gemfile.lock shows puma is already a dependency).
  2. config/environments/production.rb has the OAuth client_id AND client_secret hardcoded in git history. Lower priority than getting auth working but worth rotating the secret and moving to ENV / systemd-drop-in (same pattern used for CURRENCY_CONVERSION_WEBHOOK_URL in the recent migration).
  3. The OAuth client lives in a dead Krake/GetData.IO GCP project. Cleaner long-term: create a new OAuth 2.0 Client ID under a TrueSight-DAO-owned GCP project, retire the krake_ror_prod client.
  
  Verification: hand-off to Gary to click Sign in with Google in browser to confirm the fix works end-to-end (couldn't curl-test from outside because Devise CSRF protection blocks bare POSTs to /users/auth/google_oauth2).
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
--------

My Digital Signature:

Verify submission here: https://dapp.truesight.me/verify_request.html
